change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Minutes, publications, financial and organizational records.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
bioghist.p: Founded in 1949, the New York State Foundation cultivates, secures, and stewards financial resources to support the Cornell Cooperative Extension New York State 4-H program in developing and delivering 4-H youth development programs.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Minutes of the Board of Directors and various other committee meetings, memos, financials, scholarship and trustee files.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: New York State 4-H Foundation Records, #8663. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes executive committee minutes, proposals, memos, and financials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes original certificate of incorporation from 1949

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes executive committee minutes, proposals, memos, and financials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes executive committee minutes, memos, and financials

did.unittitle: Reports to the New York State 4-H Foundation Board of Trustees, Inventory of private fund support, report to Steering Committee
4-H National Mentoring Program Quarterly Progress Reports
Financial report and other financial documentation
Presentation "Understanding the Difference 4-H Clubs Make in the Lives of New York Youth: How 4-H Contributes to Positive Youth Development" handouts
Orientation for 4-H foundation executives resource binder
Public relations notebook for regional public relations chair
County manual on private support for 4-H
Formation of New York State 4-H Club Center at the Hyde Park Estate in Cooperstown
MOU between Cornell Cooperative Extension and 4-H Foundation
New York State 4-H Foundation publications and press releases

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "Partners With Youth" brochures and 1973 "Learning for the Future" program report.

did.unittitle: Keeping in Touch with Extension newsletter

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Presentation by Cathann Kress, Director of Youth Development at the National 4-H Headquarters

did.unittitle: Proposals organized by topic, and by county in alphabetical order A-M
Proposals organized by county in alphabetical order N-Z
Proposals for New York State Standardbred, 4-H Teen Ambassador, Finger Lakes District 4-H Agents Association, Natural Resources, Rural Sociology Department, Textiles and Apparel and Vegetable Crops.
National 4-HCouncil Youth Voice: Youth Choice sub-grant
